meaning: vt.使引起轰动 以耸人听闻的手法处理
examples: When will TOI stop trying to sensationalize news.
So it could be typical of the American media to unnecessarily sensationalize things.
People tend to want to sensationalize the images and thereby make my clients look like they are ostentatious.
Dalal said investigators did not have many details but did not want to sensationalize the discovery.
At worst, as is suggested in Deadspin, he was an accomplice looking to sensationalize a situation for personal gain.
